steps.title: Step 1
steps.instruction: Sew a Fabric A square to the end of each print rectangle. Press toward the rectangle. Repeat to make ninety-six units like this, each measuring 4-1/2” x 13-1/2”.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew a Fabric A square to the end of each print rectangle. Press toward the rectangle. Repeat to make ninety-six units like this, each measuring 4-1/2” x 13-1/2”.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.instruction: Arrange the blocks into six rows of sixteen units. Note the block orientation in the Quilt Assembly Diagram. Arrange the fabrics as shown in the Diagram, or in a random placement of your choosing.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Arrange the blocks into six rows of sixteen units. Note the block orientation in the Quilt Assembly Diagram. Arrange the fabrics as shown in the Diagram, or in a random placement of your choosing.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.instruction: Sew the units together to form rows. Press the rows to the left in the odd numbered rows and to the right in the even numbered rows.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew the units together to form rows. Press the rows to the left in the odd numbered rows and to the right in the even numbered rows.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.instruction: Sew the rows together to form the quilt center. Press the row seams open. Your quilt top is complete! Baste, quilt, bind and enjoy! From Fabrics B - O, cut: six or seven 4-1/2” x 9-1/2” rectangles, for a total of ninety-six rectangles From Fabric A, cut: eleven 4-1/2” x WOF strips. Subcut: ninety-six 4-1/2” squares From the Binding Fabric, cut: eight 2-1/2” x WOF strips Cutting Instructions
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew the rows together to form the quilt center. Press the row seams open. Your quilt top is complete! Baste, quilt, bind and enjoy!
